title: planktonr
text: planktonr package provides access plankton biogeochemical data collected Integrated Marine Observing System (IMOS) around Australia. vignette introduces main data sources demonstrates visualise sampling effort taxonomic coverage.

title: planktonr
text: NRS network consists nine coastal monitoring stations around Australia collect plankton, biogeochemical, physical oceanographic data. stations sample monthly using: Niskin bottles - phytoplankton (0.5-5 L samples 0-50 m depths) Vertical net tows - zooplankton (100 µm mesh, 0-50 m integrated samples) Stations include: Darwin (DAR), Yongala (YON), North Stradbroke Island (NSI), Port Hacking (PHB), Maria Island (MAI), Rottnest Island (ROT), Esperance (ESP), Ningaloo (NIN), Kangaroo Island (KAI). stations limited sampling: * Darwin samples quarterly capture monsoonal variability * Kangaroo Island samples 5-6 times per year due weather constraints * Esperance Ningaloo discontinued initial sampling periods

title: planktonr
text: CPR high-speed plankton sampler towed behind ships opportunity ~10 m depth. samples plankton continuously, sample representing approximately 3 nautical miles (~5.6 km). CPR primarily samples : * Great Barrier Reef region * East Australian Current region * Bass Strait South-east region CPR sampling quarterly main areas interest ad-hoc regions. Unlike NRS samples collected daylight hours, CPR samples collected day night ships operate continuously.

title: planktonr
text: SOTS mooring south Tasmania provides high-frequency physical biogeochemical measurements, intermittent biological sampling similar NRS protocols.

title: planktonr
text: sampling National Reference Stations predominantly monthly. Darwin samples quarterly basis completes multiple trips across tidal cycle. Kangaroo Island sampling restricted 5-6 times per year sea conditions. Continuous Plankton Recorder samples quarterly main areas interest (Great Barrier Reef East Australian Current) ad-hoc basis regions, depending ship availability.
code: datCPR <- pr_get_trips(Survey = "CPR") datNRS <- pr_get_trips(Survey = "NRS")  ggCPR <- planktonr::pr_plot_Gantt(datCPR) ggNRS <- planktonr::pr_plot_Gantt(datNRS)  wrap_plots(ggCPR, ggNRS, ncol = 1) &   theme(text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

what: Plot the spatial distribution of NRS and CPR samples
title: planktonr
text: National Reference Stations situated throughout coastal region Australia, Marine Bioregions least one station. Western Australian stations Ningaloo Esperance discontinued initial sampling period due logistical constraints. CPR tracks also sparse Western Australia shipping options limited. CPR sampling concentrated along major shipping routes, particularly east coast Bass Strait.

what: What functional groups do we commonly see?
title: planktonr
text: can learn lot community structure plankton examining functional groups. pie charts also show sampling methods impact find samples. Functional groups represent taxa similar ecological roles morphological characteristics. example: * Phytoplankton: Centric diatoms, pennate diatoms, dinoflagellates, cyanobacteria * Zooplankton: Copepods, appendicularians, chaetognaths, molluscs

previous_headings: What functional groups do we commonly see?
title: planktonr
text: Centric diatoms dominate NRS samples, whilst pennate diatoms become important CPR samples. likely reflects differences sampling depth method: NRS: Niskin bottle samples discrete depths (0-50 m), collected daylight CPR: Continuous sampling ~10 m depth, collected day night
code: p1 <- pr_plot_PieFG(pr_get_FuncGroups(Survey = "NRS", Type = "Phytoplankton")) p2 <- pr_plot_PieFG(pr_get_FuncGroups(Survey = "CPR", Type = "Phytoplankton"))  (wrap_plots(p1, p2, guides = "collect", ncol = 2) &     theme(text = element_text(size = 16), legend.position = "bottom",            plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")))

previous_headings: What functional groups do we commonly see?
title: planktonr
text: Copepods dominate CPR NRS net samples, typical marine zooplankton communities. However, relative abundance groups differs: Chaetognaths (arrow worms) cladocerans (water fleas) sampled effectively CPR Molluscs (primarily pteropods) caught frequently NRS net samples differences reflect different mesh sizes sampling methods: * NRS: 100 µm mesh net, vertical tows (0-50 m) * CPR: Silk mesh (~270 µm), horizontal tow ~10 m
code: p3 <- pr_plot_PieFG(pr_get_FuncGroups(Survey = "NRS", Type = "Zooplankton")) p4 <- pr_plot_PieFG(pr_get_FuncGroups(Survey = "CPR", Type = "Zooplankton"))  (wrap_plots(p3, p4, guides = "collect", ncol = 2) &     theme(text = element_text(size = 16), legend.position = "bottom",            plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")))

what: How many taxa are we identifying?
title: planktonr
text: course surveys since 2009, taxonomic resolution improved analyst training exposure diverse assemblages. now identifying: >600 phytoplankton taxa CPR >1000 zooplankton taxa CPR, including life stage sex many taxa Zooplankton diversity generally higher phytoplankton diversity : * Zooplankton larger morphological differences species definitive * Zooplankton identification includes life stages (nauplii, copepodites, adults) sex * Zooplankton sampling captures wider size range organisms

title: planktonr

what: Analysis and Visualisation of Plankton Data
title: Analysis and Visualisation of Plankton Data
text: planktonr R package facilitates download, analysis, visualisation phytoplankton zooplankton data. package primarily focuses plankton data Australian Ocean Data Network (AODN) collected Integrated Marine Observing System (IMOS), including: National Reference Stations (NRS) - Coastal monitoring stations monthly sampling Continuous Plankton Recorder (CPR) - Ship-based transect sampling across ocean basins Southern Ocean Time Series (SOTS) - Deep ocean mooring south Tasmania package provides access phytoplankton, zooplankton, larval fish, biogeochemical data, along tools trend analysis, biodiversity assessment, data visualisation.

title: Analysis and Visualisation of Plankton Data
text: package active development. can install development version GitHub : optional features require additional packages:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("PlanktonTeam/planktonr") # For satellite data access install.packages("thredds")  # For parallel processing (speeds up large data downloads) install.packages(c("future", "furrr"))  # For community analysis functions install.packages("vegan")

title: Analysis and Visualisation of Plankton Data
code: library(planktonr) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)  # Get zooplankton biomass from NRS stations df <- pr_get_Indices("NRS", "Zooplankton") %>%   filter(Parameters == "Biomass_mgm3",           StationCode %in% c("MAI", "PHB"))  # Plot time series pr_plot_TimeSeries(df, trans = "log10")  # Examine long-term trends pr_plot_Trends(df, Trend = "Raw", method = "lm", trans = "log10")  # Look at seasonal patterns pr_plot_Trends(df, Trend = "Month", method = "loess", trans = "log10")

title: Analysis and Visualisation of Plankton Data
text: pr_get_NRSData() - Raw taxonomic data NRS stations pr_get_CPRData() - Raw taxonomic data CPR surveys pr_get_Indices() - Pre-calculated ecological indices (biomass, diversity, abundance) pr_get_FuncGroups() - Functional group abundance (diatoms, copepods, etc.) pr_get_NRSChemistry() - Nutrient carbon chemistry data pr_get_NRSPigments() - Photosynthetic pigment concentrations

title: Analysis and Visualisation of Plankton Data
text: pr_plot_TimeSeries() - Basic time series plots pr_plot_Trends() - Time series fitted trend lines pr_plot_Climatology() - Seasonal annual climatologies pr_plot_tsfg() - Functional group composition time pr_plot_EOVs() - Essential Ocean Variable plots reporting

title: Define the constructor function for the planktonr data class — planktonr_dat
code: # Create from a tibble (existing behavior) tibble_data <- tibble::tibble(   time = as.Date(c("2023-01-01", "2023-01-02")),   value = c(10, 20) ) pr_obj_tibble <- planktonr_dat(   .data = tibble_data,   Type = "Phytoplankton",   Survey = "NRS",   Variable = "Chlorophyll_a" ) print(pr_obj_tibble) #> --- planktonr_dat Attributes --- #>   Type: Phytoplankton #>   Survey: NRS #>   Variable: Chlorophyll_a #>  #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   time       value #>   <date>     <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01    10 #> 2 2023-01-02    20  # Create from a base R data.frame (newly supported) df_data <- data.frame(   time = as.Date(c("2023-01-03", "2023-01-04")),   value = c(30, 40) ) pr_obj_df <- planktonr_dat(   .data = df_data,   Type = "Zooplankton",   Survey = "CPR",   Variable = "Biomass" ) print(pr_obj_df) #> --- planktonr_dat Attributes --- #>   Type: Zooplankton #>   Survey: CPR #>   Variable: Biomass #>  #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   time       value #>   <date>     <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-03    30 #> 2 2023-01-04    40 class(pr_obj_df) # Still a planktonr_dat (which inherits from tibble) #> [1] "planktonr_dat" "tbl_df"        "tbl"           "data.frame"

text: dat dataframe containing columns Longitude Latitude geographic coordinates decimal degrees (WGS84) near_dist_km Buffer distance (kilometres) use assigning bioregions samples fall outside boundaries. Default 0 (buffer). Typical values: 0 - Exact match , samples outside boundaries labelled "None" 50 - Assigns samples within 50 km bioregion 250 - generous buffer, useful CPR transects near boundaries

title: Assign Australian Marine Bioregions to sample locations — pr_add_Bioregions
text: function assigns samples one following Australian marine bioregions: Temperate East: Coastal waters NSW southern Queensland South-east: Bass Strait waters Tasmania South-west: Southern western Australian waters North-west: Waters northwest Western Australia Coral Sea: Offshore waters northeast Queensland None: Samples fall within bioregion

title: Assign Australian Marine Bioregions to sample locations — pr_add_Bioregions
text: function uses spatial operations : Convert coordinates spatial features (sf objects) Match samples bioregions using polygon intersection samples outside boundaries, find nearest bioregion within near_dist_km Prioritise Coral Sea assignments handle boundary overlaps Add colour column plotting consistency

title: Assign Australian Marine Bioregions to sample locations — pr_add_Bioregions
text: near_dist_km parameter particularly useful : CPR samples collected near bioregion boundaries Offshore samples may fall just outside defined regions Creating continuous coverage transect data Use larger buffers (e.g., 250 km) offshore CPR data, smaller buffer coastal NRS data.

title: Assign Australian Marine Bioregions to sample locations — pr_add_Bioregions
text: Input dataframe must include Longitude Latitude columns. function handles coordinate transformations automatically.

title: Assign Australian Marine Bioregions to sample locations — pr_add_Bioregions
code: # Add bioregions with exact matching (no buffer) dat <- pr_get_Raw("cpr_derived_indices_data") %>%   pr_add_Bioregions()  # Add bioregions with 250 km buffer for offshore samples dat <- pr_get_Raw("cpr_derived_indices_data") %>%   pr_add_Bioregions(near_dist_km = 250)  # Check bioregion assignments table(dat$BioRegion) #>  #>             Coral Sea                  None                 North  #>                  4105                  1108                   348  #>            North-west            South-east            South-west  #>                  1119                 11671                  3620  #> Southern Ocean Region        Temperate East  #>                  7783                  7065

title: Extract tidy model coefficients for trend analysis — pr_get_coeffs
text: function uses broom::tidy() extract coefficients model adds significance indicators: *** p ≤ 0.001 (highly significant) ** p ≤ 0.01 (significant) * p ≤ 0.05 (significant) (blank) p > 0.05 (significant) NRS/LTM data, station codes automatically added station names used identifiers.

title: Extract tidy model coefficients for trend analysis — pr_get_coeffs
text: Year_Local: rate change per year (slope). Positive = increasing, negative = decreasing pr_harmonic(Month, k = 1)1: Sine component seasonal cycle pr_harmonic(Month, k = 1)2: Cosine component seasonal cycle Intercept: Expected value Year = 0 (often interpretable) Year_Local coefficient typically interest detecting long-term trends. Significant harmonic terms indicate strong seasonal cycle.

title: Aggregate plankton data by functional groups — pr_get_FuncGroups
text: function aggregates phytoplankton five major groups: Centric diatoms - Radially symmetrical diatoms, typically bloom-forming Pennate diatoms - Bilaterally symmetrical diatoms Dinoflagellates - Flagellated protists, toxic Cyanobacteria - Photosynthetic bacteria (e.g., Synechococcus, Trichodesmium) - remaining groups (flagellates, ciliates, etc.)

title: Aggregate plankton data by functional groups — pr_get_FuncGroups
text: function aggregates zooplankton seven major groups: Copepods - Dominant group marine zooplankton Appendicularians - Gelatinous filter feeders (larvaceans) Molluscs - Primarily pteropods (sea butterflies, sea angels) Cladocerans - Water fleas (e.g., Penilia, Evadne) Chaetognaths - Arrow worms (predatory) Thaliaceans - Gelatinous filter feeders (salps, doliolids, pyrosomes) - remaining groups Data based higher taxonomic group (htg) abundance aggregated sample. Port Hacking 4 samples excluded NRS data.

title: Aggregate plankton data by functional groups — pr_get_FuncGroups
code: # Get NRS zooplankton functional groups NRSfgz <- pr_get_FuncGroups(Survey = "NRS", Type = "Zooplankton")  # Get NRS phytoplankton functional groups NRSfgp <- pr_get_FuncGroups(Survey = "NRS", Type = "Phytoplankton")  # Get CPR functional groups with expanded bioregion boundaries CPRfgz <- pr_get_FuncGroups(Survey = "CPR", Type = "Zooplankton", near_dist_km = 250) CPRfgp <- pr_get_FuncGroups(Survey = "CPR", Type = "Phytoplankton")  # Examine the functional groups present unique(NRSfgp$Parameters) #> [1] Centric diatom Pennate diatom Dinoflagellate Cyanobacteria  Other          #> Levels: Centric diatom Pennate diatom Dinoflagellate Cyanobacteria Other

what: Get plankton and biogeochemical data from IMOS surveys — pr_get_data
title: Get plankton and biogeochemical data from IMOS surveys — pr_get_data
text: unified interface retrieving plankton abundance, biovolume, biogeochemical data IMOS marine monitoring programs. function consolidates multiple survey-specific functions single entry point.

title: Get plankton and biogeochemical data from IMOS surveys — pr_get_data
code: pr_get_data(   Survey = "NRS",   Type = "Phytoplankton",   Variable = NULL,   Subset = NULL,   Format = "all" )

title: Get plankton and biogeochemical data from IMOS surveys — pr_get_data
text: Survey Survey program retrieve data : "NRS" - National Reference Stations (default). Coastal monitoring stations around Australia monthly sampling. "CPR" - Continuous Plankton Recorder. Ship-towed sampler providing broad-scale spatial coverage along shipping routes. "SOTS" - Southern Ocean Time Series. Deep-water mooring site south Tasmania monitoring Southern Ocean ecosystems. "Coastal" - Coastal Seas microbial monitoring sites. "GO-SHIP" - Global Ocean Ship-based Hydrographic Investigations Program. "HAB" - Phytoplankton monitoring data state based seafood industry monitoring programs Type Data type retrieve (case-insensitive Phytoplankton/Zooplankton): "Phytoplankton" - Phytoplankton abundance biovolume (NRS, CPR, SOTS) "Zooplankton" - Zooplankton abundance (NRS, CPR, SOTS) "Chemistry" - Water chemistry including nutrients (NRS, Coastal) "Pigments" - HPLC photosynthetic pigments (NRS ) "Pico" - Picophytoplankton flow cytometry (NRS ) "Micro" - Microbial community data (NRS, Coastal, GO-SHIP) "TSS" - Total Suspended Solids (NRS ) "CTD" - CTD profiles: temperature, salinity, fluorescence (NRS ) Variable Variable retrieve (required Phytoplankton/Zooplankton): "abundance" - Cell individual counts "biovolume" - Cell biovolume cubic micrometres (Phytoplankton ) Subset Data aggregation level (required Phytoplankton/Zooplankton): "raw" - Raw taxonomic data identified analysts "htg" - Higher taxonomic groups (e.g., diatoms, dinoflagellates) "genus" - Aggregated genus level "species" - Aggregated species level "copepods" - Copepod data (Zooplankton ) Format Output format Pigments data: "" - individual pigment concentrations (default) "binned" - Pigments grouped functional classes

title: Get plankton and biogeochemical data from IMOS surveys — pr_get_data
text: dataframe containing requested data. Structure varies data type (see Details).

title: Get plankton and biogeochemical data from IMOS surveys — pr_get_data
text: structure returned dataframe varies data type: Wide format (taxa/variables columns): Phytoplankton, Zooplankton: Taxa names columns, samples rows CTD: Measurement variables columns, depth bins rows Long format (Parameters Values columns): Chemistry, Pigments, Pico, Micro, TSS: Standardised long format Parameters column containing variable names Values column containing measurements

title: Get plankton and biogeochemical data from IMOS surveys — pr_get_data
text: Variable Subset required Phytoplankton Zooplankton Format used Pigments (defaults "") Unused parameters generate warning

title: Get plankton and biogeochemical data from IMOS surveys — pr_get_data
code: # Get NRS phytoplankton abundance data at genus level dat <- pr_get_data(Survey = "NRS", Type = "Phytoplankton",                    Variable = "abundance", Subset = "genus")  # Get CPR zooplankton data dat <- pr_get_data(Survey = "CPR", Type = "Zooplankton",                    Variable = "abundance", Subset = "htg")  # Get NRS water chemistry dat <- pr_get_data(Survey = "NRS", Type = "Chemistry")  # Get NRS pigments in binned format dat <- pr_get_data(Survey = "NRS", Type = "Pigments", Format = "binned")  # Get NRS CTD profiles dat <- pr_get_data(Survey = "NRS", Type = "CTD")  # Get GO-SHIP microbial data dat <- pr_get_data(Survey = "GO-SHIP", Type = "Micro")  # Get Coastal Seas chemistry data dat <- pr_get_data(Survey = "Coastal", Type = "Chemistry")  # Get HAB phytoplankton data dat <- pr_get_data(Survey = "HAB", Type = "Phytoplankton",                    Variable = "abundance", Subset = "genus")

text: Survey Survey type retrieve trips : "NRS" - National Reference Station trips (default) "CPR" - Continuous Plankton Recorder trips ... Additional arguments passed pr_add_Bioregions() Survey = "CPR". Currently supports: near_dist_km - Distance kilometres pad bioregion boundaries assigning samples regions (default behaviour uses exact boundaries)

title: Get sampling trip metadata — pr_get_trips
text: dataframe trip information. Common columns include: TripCode - Unique trip identifier SampleTime_Local - Local sampling date time Year_Local, Month_Local - Temporal components Latitude, Longitude - Geographic coordinates Additional columns vary survey: NRS: StationCode, StationName CPR: BioRegion - Australian marine bioregion assignment

title: Get sampling trip metadata — pr_get_trips
text: NRS Trips: Returns information NRS sampling trip (voyage), including trip code, station, date/time, basic metadata. NRS project trips included (SOTS trips excluded). Samples designated 'P' (plankton) samples sample type designation included. CPR Trips: CPR samples continuously towed behind ships opportunity. "trip" represents segment tow, typically covering approximately 3 nautical miles. Samples automatically assigned Australian marine bioregions using pr_add_Bioregions(). Bioregions include: Temperate East, South-east, South-west, North, North-west.

text: function fits linear models form: Values ~ Year + sin(Month) + cos(Month) : Year term captures long-term linear trends (increasing decreasing time) Harmonic terms capture seasonal cycles using Fourier basis functions (k=1, allowing one complete seasonal cycle per year) model fitted separately station (NRS/LTM) bioregion (CPR). text: df dataframe pr_get_Indices() containing timeseries data Trend temporal scale trend analysis: "Raw" - Plot data points time trend line full timeseries "Month" - Monthly climatology showing seasonal patterns (averaged across years) "Year" - Annual means showing interannual variability method Smoothing method trend line. Options include: "lm" - Linear regression (default, good detecting long-term trends) "loess" - Local polynomial regression (good non-linear trends) "gam" - Generalised additive model (requires mgcv package) method accepted ggplot2::geom_smooth() trans Transformation y-axis scale: "identity" - transformation (default) "log10" - Log base 10 transformation (useful abundance data) "sqrt" - Square root transformation transformation accepted ggplot2::scale_y_continuous()

text: ggplot2 object showing timeseries fitted trend line(s)

title: Plot temporal trends in plankton data with fitted lines — pr_plot_Trends
text: function designed help identify temporal trends plankton data: Raw trends: Shows observations time smoothed trend line. Useful detecting long-term changes (e.g., increasing decreasing abundance). Monthly trends: Aggregates data month across years show seasonal patterns (e.g., winter blooms, summer stratification effects). Annual trends: Shows year--year variability, useful detecting regime shifts responses climate oscillations (e.g., ENSO). shaded ribbon around trend lines represents 95% confidence interval. NRS data, values averaged across depths present.

title: Plot temporal trends in plankton data with fitted lines — pr_plot_Trends
code: # Examine long-term trends in zooplankton biomass df <- pr_get_Indices("NRS", "Zooplankton") %>%   dplyr::filter(Parameters == "Biomass_mgm3") %>%   pr_model_data() pr_plot_Trends(df, Trend = "Raw", method = "lm") #> Warning: Removed 61 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range #> (`stat_smooth()`). #> Warning: Removed 63 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).   # Examine seasonal patterns pr_plot_Trends(df, Trend = "Month", method = "loess") #> Warning: Removed 17 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).   # Examine interannual variability pr_plot_Trends(df, Trend = "Year", method = "lm") #> Warning: Removed 8 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_smooth()`). #> Warning: Removed 9 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).   # Use log transformation for abundance data df <- pr_get_Indices("CPR", "Phytoplankton", near_dist_km = 250) %>%   dplyr::filter(Parameters == "PhytoAbundance_Cellsm3",                 BioRegion %in% c("South-east", "Temperate East")) pr_plot_Trends(df, Trend = "Raw", method = "lm", trans = "log10") #> Warning: Removed 15180 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range #> (`stat_smooth()`). #> Warning: Removed 15180 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

text: Phytoplankton (5 groups): Centric diatoms (radially symmetrical, bloom-forming) Pennate diatoms (bilaterally symmetrical) Dinoflagellates (flagellated protists) Cyanobacteria (photosynthetic bacteria) (remaining groups) Zooplankton (7 groups): Copepods (dominant marine zooplankton) Appendicularians (larvaceans, gelatinous filter feeders) Molluscs (pteropods - sea butterflies angels) Cladocerans (water fleas, e.g., Penilia, Evadne) Chaetognaths (arrow worms, predatory) Thaliaceans (salps, doliolids, pyrosomes) (remaining groups)

title: Time series plot of functional group composition — pr_plot_tsfg
text: Actual Scale: Shows true abundance patterns. Useful seeing: Total community biomass/abundance changes Bloom events groups dominate numerically Proportion Scale: Shows relative composition. Useful seeing: Community shifts (e.g., diatoms dinoflagellates) Seasonal succession patterns Long-term regime shifts Changes might masked overall abundance changes Colours assigned consistently across plots functional group.

title: Remove statistical outliers from plankton data — pr_remove_outliers

title: Remove statistical outliers from plankton data — pr_remove_outliers
code: # Remove outliers using 2 SD threshold (recommended) df <- pr_get_Indices("NRS", "Zooplankton") %>%   pr_remove_outliers(2)  # More conservative removal (3 SD) df <- pr_get_Indices("CPR", "Phytoplankton") %>%   pr_remove_outliers(3)  # Check how many values were removed df_before <- pr_get_EOVs("NRS") %>%   dplyr::filter(Parameters == "Biomass_mgm3") df_after <- df_before %>% pr_remove_outliers(2) nrow(df_before) - nrow(df_after) #> [1] 117
